meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master Git and AI to launch your first SaaS. A beginner-friendly, step-by-step checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development.
content.answer_block: You don't need years of experience to launch a SaaS; you need a modern AI-assisted workflow. By integrating Git version control with Claude Code and Antigravity, you automate the complex scaffolding and debugging processes, allowing you to focus on product logic while maintaining professional-grade code architecture from day one.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of ambition. You stare at the screen, paralyzed by the gap between your vision and the crushing complexity of modern full-stack development. Most students quit here, convinced that 'SaaS knowledge' is a secret club they aren't invited to join.

But the rules of the game have shifted. With AI-native tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you are no longer just a student coding in isolation; you are an architect commanding a fleet of specialized agents. This guide is your bridge from 'I have an idea' to 'It's live in production'.

steps.title: Initialize Your Intelligent Repository
steps.description: Establish a professional Git structure immediately. AI-assisted development requires clean branch management to prevent the 'spaghetti code' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS project structure using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Set up a .gitignore for sensitive keys and organize folders into /app, /components, and /lib.

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data model with AI before writing a single line of application code to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS user dashboard. Include tables for profiles, subscriptions, and usage logs. Provide the SQL migration scripts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create database migration for user profiles and subscription tiers'

steps.title: Automating Feature Development
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate requirements into functional modules, keeping your Git commits granular and atomic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a secure authentication flow using Supabase Auth. Ensure the code is modular, well-commented, and follows modern React patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement email/password auth and protect the /dashboard route'

steps.title: Debugging with AI-Context
steps.description: When errors occur, use your AI tool to analyze the Git diff and identify the exact source of failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current Git diff and identify why the authentication middleware is failing to redirect unauthenticated users.

steps.title: Deployment and Version Integrity
steps.description: Prepare your code for production by creating a release branch and automating the final build check.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks before production deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'perform a security audit and prepare build scripts for Vercel'

pro_tips: Always commit to a feature branch before asking your AI to refactor, so you can roll back instantly if the AI hallucinates a breaking change.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests first; having a green test suite makes AI-assisted coding significantly more reliable.
Treat your AI prompt history as documentation; keep your prompts descriptive to 'train' the AI on your specific coding style over time.
Never let the AI push directly to main; always review diffs manually to ensure you understand the architecture it is building for you.
Use AI to generate your README.md files; it forces you to articulate the 'Why' behind your project, which is essential for your portfolio.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is live, clone your repository and attempt to build a secondary feature using a different tech stack to test your newfound architectural confidence.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect time' to learn. You have the tools, the guide, and the blueprint to ship your vision today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
